qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unrestricted version negotiation response enables UDP reflection and amplification attacks
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a panic in QUIC transport parameter parsing due to unwrap() on fallible varint decode at transport_parameters.rs:473, causing crash/DoS. The finding describes a completely different vulnerability: unrestricted version negotiation responses at endpoint.rs:164-196 enabling DDoS reflection/amplification. Different code locations, different root causes (unwrap panic vs missing rate limiting), different impacts (crash vs amplification attack), different vulnerability classes (CWE-248 panic vs amplification).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-6xvm-j4wr-6v98) describes a remote unauthenticated denial of service via panic in QUIC transport parameter parsing at transport_parameters.rs:473, where unwrap() is called on fallible varint decode. The single qualifying finding (version_negotiation_amplification) describes a different vulnerability: unrestricted version negotiation responses at endpoint.rs:164-196 enabling DDoS reflection/amplification attacks. These are distinct vulnerabilities with different root causes, code locations, and impact mechanisms. No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.
evidence: Advisory root cause: unwrap() on Err value in transport parameter varint decoding (transport_parameters.rs:473)
Advisory impact: Panic/crash on malformed transport parameters (CWE-248)
Finding root cause: Missing rate limiting on version negotiation response path (endpoint.rs:164-196)
No code location or mechanism overlap between advisory and finding
